result:
Data cleaning completed successfully. The R script:

1. Loaded all 4 wave files from data/input/ using readr::read_delim() with tab delimiter
2. Merged datasets using full_join by NSID (the original ID variable)
3. Renamed wave-specific variables to lowercase format (lang_S1, lang_S2, lang_S3, lang_S4)
4. Harmonized missing value codes to standard codes (-9=Refusal, -8=Don't know, -1=Not applicable, -3=Not asked, -2=Schedule not applicable)
5. Created consolidated 'lang' variable using prioritization logic for time-invariant characteristics (earliest valid positive value first, then earliest missing code as fallback)
6. Output contains only NSID and lang columns (no intermediate wave-specific variables)
7. Saved to data/output/cleaned_data.csv

Final output: 16,111 rows × 2 columns (NSID, lang)
Language variable distribution: 12,453 English only (1), 1,873 English first/main + other languages (2), 1,122 Other language (3), 468 Bilingual (4), 194 Not asked (-3), 1 Don't know (-8)
